description: The reference library DATA (catalog of analysed sites, index.json, the curation queue) lives in a public GitHub repo, NOT inside this skill. A skill is capped at ~200 files; a growing library of deeply-captured sites - tens of files each - would blow that cap. The skill keeps the machinery (scripts, schema, docs); the repo holds the data and grows without limit.
howToUse: Run scripts/sync-reference-library.sh - it clones or updates the repo into cacheDir and prints an export line for JIFFI_REFERENCE_LIBRARY_PATH. select-references.sh and add-reference-site.sh then operate on the local clone exactly as before.

auth.reads: None. The repo is public - cloning and reading need no credentials.
auth.writes: Set the JIFFI_GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable to a GitHub token that has Contents:write on this repo. sync-reference-library.sh picks it up and authenticates pushes with it.
auth.SECURITY: NEVER paste a token into this file, or any file inside the skill or the library repo. The skill is distributed and installed by others, and the repo is public - a committed token is an immediate credential leak. Keep the token only in your shell environment, or in a local file that is git-ignored and lives OUTSIDE the skill (e.g. ~/.config/jiffi/website-references.token, then `export JIFFI_GITHUB_TOKEN=$(cat ~/.config/jiffi/website-references.token)`). For unattended curation, the cleanest option is to run it from Claude Code on a machine whose git is already authenticated, so no token is handled at all.

note: If you move or rename the repo, update only the repo field above.
